Batch 3: Batch 3 executed successfully. T4 is complete: all required pytest runs passed, strengthened verification confirmed the spawn-failure path maps RunPod `status == error` to `final_status = terminated` in both `_spawn_worker` implementations, and the batch checkpoint was updated at `.megaplan/plans/fix-gpu-orchestrator-scaling-20260330-1936/execution_batch_3.json`.
commands_run: rg -n "gpu_idle_timeout_sec|early_desired = max\(|buffer_based = busy_count \+ config\.machines_to_keep_idle|desired_floor = max\(|max_by_buffer = max\(0, decision\.idle_count - config\.machines_to_keep_idle\)|mark_worker_error\(worker_id, 'Failed to spawn Runpod instance'\)|mark_worker_error\(worker_id, \"Failed to spawn Runpod instance\"\)|_calculate_scaling_decision_legacy|_failsafe_stale_worker_check|if ws\.should_terminate: continue" gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py gpu_orchestrator/worker_state.py gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/health.py gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/lifecycle.py gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/worker_capacity.py gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/scaling_decision.py gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/periodic.py
rg -n "mark_worker_error|_is_worker_idle_with_timeout|Failed basic health check with tasks queued|heartbeat|stuck" gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/health.py gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py

rg -n "Idle healthy workers are PATH B's responsibility|ws\.is_active and not ws\.has_active_task and ws\.heartbeat_is_recent|spawning_timeout_sec|gpu_idle_timeout_sec" gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/periodic.py gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py
rg -n "spawn_failed|Failed to spawn Runpod instance" gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/worker_capacity.py

task_updates.executor_notes: Applied the requested Phase 1 fixes in both the legacy control_loop.py path and the corresponding phase mixins: removed PATH A idle-timeout termination, enforced max(config.machines_to_keep_idle, 1) in desired_floor/buffer/early_desired/scale-down buffer calculations, changed spawn-failure handling from error to terminated with termination_reason=spawn_failed, and added the DECISION-003 TODO comment to control_loop.py legacy scaling. Verification: greps show scaling/termination logic now uses max(..., 1) everywhere relevant, health.py no longer contains the idle-timeout termination block, and spawn failures no longer call mark_worker_error.
commands_run: rg -n "mark_worker_error|_is_worker_idle_with_timeout|Failed basic health check with tasks queued|heartbeat|stuck" gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/health.py gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py
rg -n "spawn_failed|Failed to spawn Runpod instance" gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/worker_capacity.py

task_updates.executor_notes: Added the healthy-idle worker guard to both failsafe implementations so PATH C skips recent-heartbeat idle workers and leaves them to PATH B scaling execution. Verification: both control_loop.py and periodic.py now continue on ws.is_active and not ws.has_active_task and ws.heartbeat_is_recent, while stale detection still uses spawning_timeout_sec for spawning workers and gpu_idle_timeout_sec for genuinely stale/lost-heartbeat workers.
commands_run: rg -n "Idle healthy workers are PATH B's responsibility|ws\.is_active and not ws\.has_active_task and ws\.heartbeat_is_recent|spawning_timeout_sec|gpu_idle_timeout_sec" gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/periodic.py gpu_orchestrator/control_loop.py

task_updates.executor_notes: Updated the scaling-decision tests to match the enforced warm-capacity floor, added the two required machines_to_keep_idle=0 scale-down/buffer cases, added a lifecycle mixin test proving early termination keeps the minimum warm buffer, and added a circuit-breaker test covering terminated spawn_failed workers. Verification: python -m pytest tests/test_scaling_decision_spawn.py tests/test_scaling_decision_limits.py tests/gpu_orchestrator/control/phases/test_lifecycle.py tests/test_circuit_breaker.py -v passed with 23 tests green.

Ran the full required verification sequence successfully with `TMPDIR=/tmp`: 20/20 passed for `tests/test_scaling_decision_spawn.py` plus `tests/test_scaling_decision_limits.py`, 21/21 passed for `tests/gpu_orchestrator/`, 1/1 passed for `tests/test_circuit_breaker.py`, and 78/78 passed for `python -m pytest tests/ -v`. Strengthened verification confirms no idle-timeout termination callers remain in the health-check path, all relevant scaling/termination uses of `machines_to_keep_idle` apply `max(config.machines_to_keep_idle, 1)`, there are no `final_status = error` assignments in either `_spawn_worker` implementation, both RunPod `status == error` branches map to `final_status = terminated` with `termination_reason=spawn_failed`, and `use_new_scaling_logic` defaults to `true` in `config.py`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Satisfied. In scaling/termination logic, desired_floor, buffer_based, early_desired, and max_by_buffer now use max(config.machines_to_keep_idle, 1) in worker_state.py, control_loop.py, lifecycle.py, scaling_decision.py, and worker_capacity.py. Remaining bare machines_to_keep_idle references are logging-only.

Satisfied. health.py no longer contains the idle-timeout mark_worker_error block. The health path still performs queued-task basic health checks, busy-worker heartbeat validation, and stuck-task detection.

Satisfied. Both failsafe implementations now skip ws.is_active and not ws.has_active_task and ws.heartbeat_is_recent, while still flagging spawning-timeout workers and workers whose heartbeat/effective age exceeds the configured cutoff.

Satisfied. The updated/new tests cover all four requested scenarios: preserving warm idle capacity with machines_to_keep_idle=0, overcapacity scale-down while still keeping one idle worker, early termination honoring max(machines_to_keep_idle, 1), and terminated spawn_failed workers not poisoning the failure-rate circuit breaker.

Satisfied. `TMPDIR=/tmp python -m pytest tests/ -v` passed with 78 tests and 0 failures. Strengthened verification confirms no idle-timeout termination callers remain in the health-check path, all relevant scaling/termination uses of `machines_to_keep_idle` apply `max(..., 1)`, and both `_spawn_worker` implementations map RunPod `status == error` to `final_status = terminated` with `termination_reason=spawn_failed` rather than any error-status worker update.
